desc: Alanya is the eastern, value-leaning end of the Turkish Riviera: a beach city wrapped around a castle peninsula, with Kleopatra Beach on one side, the Red Tower and harbour on the other, and hot-weather escape routes into caves, rivers and the Taurus foothills. It is strongest for shoulder-season sun and family-friendly resort logistics, while July and August are better treated as swim-and-siesta months than sightseeing months.

todo.name: Alanya Castle
todo.desc: Walk the Seljuk-era fortress walls for harbour views and a strong sense of Alanya's layered coastal history.

todo.name: Kleopatra Beach
todo.desc: Use the wide west-side beach for swims, sunset walks and easy access to cafes below the castle cliffs.

todo.name: Red Tower and harbour
todo.desc: Pair the octagonal Kizil Kule with the old shipyard and evening promenade around the marina.

todo.name: Dim River
todo.desc: Head inland to shaded riverside restaurants when the coast is at its hottest.

todo.name: Damlataş Cave
todo.desc: Use the cool cave near Kleopatra Beach as a short heat-break stop before or after the cable car.

todo.name: Sapadere Canyon
todo.desc: Book a canyon trip or drive inland for waterfalls and cooler mountain air when beach humidity builds.

wifi.note: Generally reliable in central hotels, apartments and seafront cafes; speeds are less predictable in hillside villas, canyon trips and older resort blocks.

prac.fltNote: Alanya is usually reached via Gazipaşa-Alanya for the shortest transfer or Antalya for the strongest UK leisure schedule. Treat destination-level LGW/LCY nonstop as false: LGW has direct Antalya gateway flights, but not a reliable nonstop into Gazipaşa-Alanya itself; Antalya adds roughly a 2-2.5 hour road transfer and peaks hard in UK school-holiday weeks.
prac.lang: Turkish; English varies outside resorts and major hotels
prac.visa: British citizens can normally visit Turkey visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period; still verify passport-validity and entry rules before booking.
bestFor: Travellers who do not mind gateway transfers
alerts: July and August are extremely hot for castle walls, canyon boardwalks and exposed harbour walks; start early or go near sunset.
Gateway choice matters: Gazipaşa is quicker but thinner on routes, while Antalya has more UK choice and a much longer transfer.
Boat-trip, beach-club and family-room prices rise sharply in Turkish and UK school-holiday weeks.
Winter rain can interrupt coastal day plans, so keep one flexible hammam, cave or market slot.

sweet: May, early June, September and October are the sweet spots for warm seas, open resort services and less punishing sightseeing heat than July-August.
